challenges.title: Widget Registry
challenges.description: Create a dictionary called 'widgets' that maps four tkinter widget names to their descriptions: 'Label' -> 'Displays text or images', 'Button' -> 'Clickable action trigger', 'Entry' -> 'Single-line text input', 'Frame' -> 'Container for organizing widgets'. Then write a function 'get_widget_info' that takes a widget name and returns its description, or 'Unknown widget' if the name is not found.
challenges.test_code:
assert len(widgets) == 4
assert widgets['Label'] == 'Displays text or images'
assert widgets['Button'] == 'Clickable action trigger'
assert widgets['Entry'] == 'Single-line text input'
assert widgets['Frame'] == 'Container for organizing widgets'
assert get_widget_info('Label') == 'Displays text or images'
assert get_widget_info('Button') == 'Clickable action trigger'
assert get_widget_info('Spinner') == 'Unknown widget'
assert get_widget_info('') == 'Unknown widget'
hints: Build the dict with the exact key-value pairs specified in the description.
Use widgets.get(name, 'Unknown widget') for safe lookup with a default.

challenges.title: Form Data Validator
challenges.description: Write a function called 'validate_form' that takes a dictionary with keys 'name', 'age', and 'email'. It should return a dictionary with key 'valid' (bool) and 'errors' (list of error strings). Validation rules: name must be a non-empty string (after stripping), age must be convertible to an integer between 0 and 150, email must contain exactly one '@' with non-empty parts on both sides. If all valid, errors list is empty.
challenges.starter_code:
def validate_form(data):
    """Validate form data dict with name, age, email fields.
    
    Returns dict with 'valid' (bool) and 'errors' (list of str).
    """
    pass
challenges.test_code:
# Valid form
result = validate_form({'name': 'Alice', 'age': '30', 'email': 'alice@example.com'})
assert result['valid'] == True
assert result['errors'] == []

# Empty name
result2 = validate_form({'name': '  ', 'age': '25', 'email': 'a@b.com'})
assert result2['valid'] == False
assert any('name' in e.lower() for e in result2['errors'])

# Bad age
result3 = validate_form({'name': 'Bob', 'age': 'abc', 'email': 'b@c.com'})
assert result3['valid'] == False
assert any('age' in e.lower() for e in result3['errors'])

# Age out of range
result4 = validate_form({'name': 'Carol', 'age': '200', 'email': 'c@d.com'})
assert result4['valid'] == False

# Bad email - no @
result5 = validate_form({'name': 'Dave', 'age': '40', 'email': 'nodomain'})
assert result5['valid'] == False
assert any('email' in e.lower() for e in result5['errors'])

# Bad email - empty parts
result6 = validate_form({'name': 'Eve', 'age': '35', 'email': '@b.com'})
assert result6['valid'] == False

# Multiple errors
result7 = validate_form({'name': '', 'age': 'xyz', 'email': 'bad'})
assert result7['valid'] == False
assert len(result7['errors']) == 3
hints: Build an errors list. Check each field and append an error message if invalid. Set valid to len(errors) == 0.
For age: use try/except around int(data['age']) to catch non-numeric. For email: use split('@') and check len(parts)==2 with both parts non-empty.
